search:Testing Angular Material Design P.content:
- Use `ng test` to run tests.
- Update Angular Material and CDK to latest versions using `npm update @angular/material @angular/cdk`.
- Configure testing in `karma.conf.js` for custom settings.
- Implement component harnesses with `@angular/cdk/testing`.
- Use `render()` from `@testing-library/angular` for role-based queries.
- Apply the Page Object pattern to encapsulate component interactions.
- Ensure tests cover all components and edge cases.

- Use `ng test` to run tests.
- Ensure Angular Material components are tested using the provided testing utilities.
- For HttpClient, use `provideRouter` in your test module.
- Update Angular and Angular Material to their latest versions for best practices and features.
- Configure `tsconfig.spec.json` for TypeScript settings specific to testing.
- Use Jest or Jasmine for writing tests, depending on preference.
- Employ mocking patterns like `HttpClientTestingModule` for HTTP requests.
- Review the official Angular documentation for the most current testing guidelines.

- Use `npm install @testing-library/react` or `yarn add @testing-library/react` to install the latest version of React Testing Library.
- For Material UI components, test without tying tests too closely to Material UI internals for better maintainability.
- Utilize Jest and React Testing Library for writing unit tests.
- Key config options include setting up a testing environment in your `package.json` or using a configuration file like `jest.config.js`.
- Example CLI command: `npm test -- --watchAll`
- Latest versions can be checked on the official Material UI GitHub repository or npm page.
- Focus on component isolation and interaction tests to ensure robustness.

- Use `npm install --save-dev @testing-library/react` to add React Testing Library.
- Run tests with `npm test`.
- Configure Jest in your project root with a `jest.config.js` file.
- Key config options include `testEnvironment: 'jsdom'`, `setupFilesAfterEnv: ['./setupTests.js']`.
- Use `@material-ui/core` for Material Design components.
- Install Material UI icons with `npm install @material-ui/icons`.
- For testing Material UI components, use `@testing-library/jest-dom` for additional matchers.
